title: Caching Required: High Read Throughput
description: Policy pattern that enforces caching when read QPS reaches 200+ req/s — the threshold at which a typical managed database instance begins saturating under direct query load.

cost.costNotes: Meta-policy pattern with no direct cost. Actual caching infrastructure costs are accounted for in the cache strategy pattern selected downstream (cache-aside, write-through-cache).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Zero cost — meta-policy pattern only. Caching infrastructure costs are in cache-aside, write-through-cache, and write-back-cache patterns.
provenance.adoptionCost: Zero adoption cost — this pattern auto-detects QPS requirements from the spec and enforces caching as a hard compilation requirement.
provenance.licenseCost: No license cost — policy enforcement pattern only.

provides.reasoning: Detects when read throughput exceeds the threshold where caching is required to protect the backing database, and enforces /constraints/features/caching: true as a hard compilation gate.

requires.reasoning: At 200+ read QPS, a cache layer is required to prevent database overload. The specific cache implementation is determined by the cache strategy pattern selected after caching is enabled.

availability.reason: General architectural enforcement pattern applicable to any high-read-throughput system

supports_nfr.reason: Pattern activates when read QPS reaches 200+ req/s — the threshold at which direct database queries typically begin saturating without a caching layer.
Pattern is compatible with any authentication mechanism; cache key design handles auth context.
Pattern is compatible with both audited and non-audited environments.
Caching supports all tenant isolation strategies through tenant-scoped cache key namespacing.
Pattern is compatible with both PII and non-PII workloads; PII requires cache encryption and appropriate TTL policies.
Compatible with GDPR when cache invalidation covers right-to-be-forgotten requirements.
Compatible with HIPAA when cache stores use encryption at rest and in transit.
Compatible with CCPA when cache invalidation handles data deletion requests.
Compatible with SOX; database remains the authoritative audit trail.
Consistency enforcement is delegated to the downstream cache strategy pattern (cache-aside requires needsReadYourWrites: false; write-through supports either).
Durability enforcement is delegated to the downstream cache strategy pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Pattern is cloud-agnostic; caching infrastructure exists on all major clouds and on-premises.
Pattern is language-agnostic; cache client libraries exist for all major languages.
High read QPS can occur on any platform type; caching enforcement is platform-agnostic.
Read caching is independent of async messaging infrastructure.
Read caching is beneficial for caching AI inference results but not limited to AI workloads.
Caching supports both single-tenant and multi-tenant architectures.
High read QPS can occur in both real-time and batch-style read workloads.
Read caching is compatible with distributed transaction systems.
High read QPS occurs in both streaming and non-streaming workloads.
Read caching can cache vector search results; pattern is not limited to vector workloads.
Read caching works with document stores as the backing database.
Read caching works with key-value stores as the backing database.
Read caching can cache graph query results.
Read caching can cache time-series aggregations or recent data points.
High read QPS occurs in both OLTP and non-OLTP workloads.
Read caching can cache OLAP query results to reduce analytical query load.

requires_constraints.reason: At 200+ read QPS, caching is mandatory to prevent database overload. Set constraints.features.caching: true

requires_nfr.reason: Caching improves availability by reducing DB load, but the combined cache+DB stack is bounded at four nines without multi-region active-active infrastructure.
Caching adds a network hop; p95 floor is ~1ms for in-memory cache hits.
Cache misses fall back to the database, adding tail latency.
Cache failures trigger immediate fallback to database; near-zero RTO is achievable.
Cache is ephemeral; database is the source of truth. RPO = 0 is achievable.
Caching infrastructure supports up to 1M req/s (3.6B/hour) with a properly sized cache cluster.
